<Meta of={AlertStories} name="Overview" />

# Alert

Displays important messages at the top of the page. Supports three types — emergency, announcement, and marketing — each with distinct visual styling to signal urgency. Alerts are dismissible and use `localStorage` to remember when a user has closed them.

## Interactive Preview

Once dismissed, an alert won't reappear until you click **Reset dismissed alerts** in the preview below.

<Canvas of={AlertStories.Alert} />
<Controls of={AlertStories.Alert} />

## Alert Types

<Canvas of={AlertStories.AlertExamples} />

<table>
  <thead>

      <td>
        <strong>Emergency</strong>
      </td>
      <td>
        Campus emergencies, system outages, urgent time-sensitive announcements
      </td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        <strong>Announcement</strong>
      </td>
      <td>Important deadlines, policy updates, scheduled maintenance</td>
    </tr>
    <tr>
      <td>
        <strong>Marketing</strong>
      </td>
      <td>
        Event promotions, new content highlights, non-critical announcements
      </td>
    </tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

## Properties

{twigPropsTable(componentProps, 'optional')}

## Behavior

- **Dismissible** — users can close alerts; state is stored in `localStorage` via the `alert__id`
- **Unique IDs required** — always provide a unique `alert__id` per alert; reusing IDs causes multiple alerts to dismiss together
- **No JS fallback** — alerts remain visible if JavaScript is disabled; the close button won't function

## Usage

- Use alerts sparingly — overuse reduces their impact
- Place alerts at the top of the page where they're immediately visible
- Keep content concise (2–3 sentences) and include a CTA link when action is needed
- Don't use multiple emergency alerts on the same page
